events.label: assistant
events.text: Findings written to `/app/answer.json`. Summary: the shared-neutral configuration means a single-pole AFCI breaker can't correctly sense/protect the circuit — the tech must de-energize, trace the neutral, confirm opposite-leg MWBC wiring, and install a listed 2-pole/common-trip AFCI (or split the neutral) before any further re-energized testing. Continuing to test on the energized shared-neutral setup is a sequence violation and a code/safety issue (210.4(B), 210.12), so `decision` is `fail` with `risk: high`.
